summary:
The most accurate way to differentiate a dentigerous cyst from an ameloblastoma is through histopathological examination, which identifies characteristic epithelial structures—such as enamel organ-like cells in ameloblastomas vs. hyperplastic dental follicle tissue in dentigerous cysts. Imaging findings like unilocular radiolucency are non-diagnostic due to overlapping presentations; neither aspirate cytology nor general clinical signs provide reliably distinct results.  

$\boxed{C}$

Imaging findings like unilocular radiolucency are non-diagnostic due to overlapping presentations; neither aspirate cytology nor general clinical signs provide reliably distinct results.  

$\boxed{C}$. Answer choice: C
